references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.title: geographies
references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.content: Type Geography Multiplicity 0..* Geographies within this market segment.

references/eClassifiers/RegulatoryConstr.title: affectedCapabilities
references/eClassifiers/RegulatoryConstr.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Capabilities affected by this regulatory constraint.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/index.title: Hypothesis
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/index.content: A testable belief that drives product decisions. Every value proposition, feature, capability, market bet, and pricing decision is ultimately based on a belief that must be validated. A Hypothesis is: - Falsifiable - Linked to a persona, need, goal, pain point, or value proposition - Validated or invalidated by evidence - Driving experiments and decisions Example: ‘We believe that enterprise hiring managers will reduce screening time by 50% if we provide Premium Skill Signals.’ Hypotheses sit before Value Propositions in the order of abstraction: Persona → Needs/Pain Points/Goals → Hypotheses → Value Propositions → Capabilities → Editions → Subscription Plans You test hypotheses → some become validated → those support Value Props → those define packaging. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: supportsValuePropositions
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type ValueProposition Multiplicity 0..* Value propositions that this hypothesis supports (once validated).

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: experienceLevel
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Level of experience, e.g. ‘Junior’, ‘Mid-level’, ‘Senior’, ‘Expert’.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: targetPersonas
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type Persona Multiplicity 0..* Personas this value proposition targets.

references/eClassifiers/Release/referenc.title: releaseDate
references/eClassifiers/Release/referenc.content: Type EDate Multiplicity 0..1 Date the release was published or made available.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: addressedNeeds
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type Need Multiplicity 0..* Needs this value proposition addresses.

references/eClassifiers/Behavior/referen.title: relatedGoals
references/eClassifiers/Behavior/referen.content: Type Goal Multiplicity 0..* Goals motivated by this behavior.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: risks
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Risks associated with this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/Version/index.ht.title: Version
references/eClassifiers/Version/index.ht.content: A version identifier. Products, features, capabilities, and capability providers may have versions. Versioning may be semantic, calendar-based, or follow other schemes. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Edition/index.ht.title: Edition
references/eClassifiers/Edition/index.ht.content: A flavor of a product with a specific set of features and capabilities. Examples: Professional Edition, Community Edition, Enterprise Edition. Editions bundle features/capabilities and are sold via subscription plans. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.title: addressedPainPoints
references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.content: Type PainPoint Multiplicity 0..* Pain points that this feature addresses or mitigates.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: personas
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type Persona Multiplicity 0..* All personas defined in this model.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: portfolios
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type ProductPortfolio Multiplicity 0..* Product portfolios in this model.

references/eClassifiers/FeaturePersonaAs.title: persona
references/eClassifiers/FeaturePersonaAs.content: Type Persona Multiplicity 1 The persona associated with the containing feature.

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: attitudes
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Attitudes and preferences of the persona, e.g. ‘risk-averse’, ‘data-driven’.

references/eClassifiers/HypothesisStatus.title: Invalidated
references/eClassifiers/HypothesisStatus.content: The hypothesis has been invalidated by evidence.

references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.title: autoRenewal
references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.content: Type EBoolean Multiplicity 0..1 Whether the subscription auto-renews.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: ValueProposition
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: A concise statement of who it is for, what it helps them achieve, and why it is better than alternatives. Treating VP as data enables: - Persona-specific messaging - Edition/plan design tied to benefits - Launch/experiment planning - Outcome measurement and iteration VP sits between user intent (needs/goals) and product packaging (editions/plans), with capabilities as the bridge and evidence as validation. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/OutcomeMetric/in.title: OutcomeMetric
references/eClassifiers/OutcomeMetric/in.content: A measurable metric tied to outcomes. Used to validate value propositions and hypotheses. Examples: activation rate, NRR, win-rate, time-to-value. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.title: pricingModel
references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Pricing model, e.g. ‘per-user’, ‘per-device’, ‘usage-based’, ‘hybrid’, ‘flat-rate’.

references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.title: supportedGoals
references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.content: Type Goal Multiplicity 0..* Goals that this feature supports or helps achieve.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: outcomeMetrics
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type OutcomeMetric Multiplicity 0..* Outcome metrics defined in this model.

references/eClassifiers/ModelElement/ind.title: ModelElement
references/eClassifiers/ModelElement/ind.content: Abstract base for every element in the product management model. Provides a common name and description. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Behavior/referen.title: category
references/eClassifiers/Behavior/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Category of behavior, e.g. ‘attitude’, ‘work-style’, ‘motivation-driver’, ‘decision-inclination’, ‘interaction-pattern’.

references/eClassifiers/Industry/index.h.title: Industry
references/eClassifiers/Industry/index.h.content: An industry classification (e.g. NAICS/SIC taxonomy). Examples: Financial Services, Healthcare, Manufacturing. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/HypothesisStatus.title: Deprecated
references/eClassifiers/HypothesisStatus.content: The hypothesis is no longer relevant.

references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.title: usedCapabilities
references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Capabilities that this feature uses or depends on.

references/eClassifiers/Edition/referenc.title: includedFeatures
references/eClassifiers/Edition/referenc.content: Type Feature Multiplicity 0..* Features included in this edition.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: enablingCapabilities
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Capabilities (including features) that enable this value proposition.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: successMetrics
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type OutcomeMetric Multiplicity 0..* Metrics used to measure success of this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.title: companySizes
references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.content: Type CompanySize Multiplicity 0..* Company size bands within this market segment.

references/eClassifiers/ServiceLevel/ref.title: metric
references/eClassifiers/ServiceLevel/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The metric or dimension, e.g. ‘response-time’, ‘token-budget’, ‘available-models’.

references/eClassifiers/GoalType/referen.title: Functional
references/eClassifiers/GoalType/referen.content: A concrete, task-oriented outcome, e.g. ‘ship releases faster’.

references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.title: IdealCustomerProfile
references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.content: A composite that constrains Segment + Industry + Size + Tech Stack to define the ideal customer. ICP is a composite of market primitives. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: linkedNeeds
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type Need Multiplicity 0..* Needs linked to this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/CapabilityProvid.title: providedCapabilities
references/eClassifiers/CapabilityProvid.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Capabilities that this provider delivers.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: capabilities
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Standalone capabilities not owned by a specific product.

references/eClassifiers/Alternative/refe.title: alternativeType
references/eClassifiers/Alternative/refe.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Type of alternative, e.g. ‘competitor’, ‘incumbent’, ‘manual-process’, ‘open-source’.

references/eClassifiers/Capability/refer.title: providers
references/eClassifiers/Capability/refer.content: Type CapabilityProvider Multiplicity 0..* Providers that can deliver this capability.

references/eClassifiers/Need/references/.title: relatedNeeds
references/eClassifiers/Need/references/.content: Type Need Multiplicity 0..* Other needs related to this need. Supports shared needs across personas.

references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.title: targetCompanySizes
references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.content: Type CompanySize Multiplicity 0..* Target company sizes for this ICP.

references/eClassifiers/FeaturePersonaAs.title: FeaturePersonaAssociation
references/eClassifiers/FeaturePersonaAs.content: An association class between a Feature and a Persona. Contained by the Feature. Allows describing how and why a feature is relevant to a specific persona. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/CompanySize/refe.title: employeeBand
references/eClassifiers/CompanySize/refe.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Employee count band, e.g. ‘1-50’, ‘51-200’, ‘201-1000’, ‘1000+’.

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: age
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type EIntegerObject Multiplicity 0..1 Representative age of the persona.

references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.title: country
references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Country name or ISO code.

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: goals
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type Goal Multiplicity 0..* Desired outcomes this persona is trying to accomplish. Goals may be functional, emotional, or aspirational.

references/eClassifiers/PainPoint/refere.title: blockedGoals
references/eClassifiers/PainPoint/refere.content: Type Goal Multiplicity 0..* Goals that this pain point blocks or hinders.

references/eClassifiers/ModelElement/ref.title: name
references/eClassifiers/ModelElement/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Human-readable name of this element.

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: workEnvironment
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Description of the persona’s work environment, e.g. ‘remote startup’, ‘enterprise office’.

index.html.title: Product Management Model
index.html.content: An Ecore model for the product management domain covering personas, needs, goals, pain points, products, features, capabilities, editions, subscriptions, value propositions, market segments, hypotheses, and more. The model supports fine-grained product management and user education, allowing both the product builder and product users to achieve maximum synergetic value. It can also be used as a runtime or build artifact to tie product usage to the model and generate reports such as popular features. Key domain areas: - Persona: fictional but research-based representation of target users with behaviors, actions, needs, goals, and pain points - Product: portfolios, products, editions, features, capabilities, and capability providers - Commercial: subscriptions and pricing - Versioning: versions and releases - Value: value propositions, evidence, alternatives, and outcome metrics - Market: segments, industries, geographies, company sizes, channels, and ideal customer profiles - Hypothesis: testable beliefs driving product decisions Edition OutcomeMetric Industry Channel availableChannels Release Subscription editions FeaturePersonaAssociation ProductPortfolio ReleaseStatus Behavior Product releases editions products Version versions version CompanySize RegulatoryConstraint regulatoryConstraints Persona behaviors targetPersonas persona Goal goals relatedGoals Need supportedGoals needs relatedNeeds PainPoint violatedNeeds blockedGoals painPoints relatedPainPoints Capability versions affectedCapabilities includedCapabilities GoalType ServiceLevel serviceLevels Feature personaAssociations usedCapabilities addressedPainPoints addressedNeeds supportedGoals features includedFeatures includedFeatures ProductManagementModel capabilities personas subscriptions products outcomeMetrics portfolios CapabilityProvider versions providedCapabilities requiredCapabilities capabilityProviders providers Action relatedCapabilities relatedFeatures actions Evidence measuredMetrics evidence Geography regulatoryConstraints availableGeographies HypothesisStatus IdealCustomerProfile targetIndustries targetGeographies targetCompanySizes targetPersonas Alternative alternatives MarketSegment channels industries geographies idealCustomerProfiles companySizes targetPersonas collectedInSegments marketSegments Hypothesis enabledByCapabilities linkedPainPoints targetPersonas linkedNeeds linkedGoals evidence successMetrics hypotheses ValueProposition differentiatedFromAlternatives enablingCapabilities mitigatedPainPoints targetPersonas addressedNeeds supportedGoals targetSegments packagedInEditions supportingEvidence supportsValuePropositions valuePropositions Product Management

references/eClassifiers/Industry/referen.title: regulatoryConstraints
references/eClassifiers/Industry/referen.content: Type RegulatoryConstraint Multiplicity 0..* Regulatory constraints applicable to this industry.

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: actions
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type Action Multiplicity 0..* Concrete, observable steps this persona performs in workflows or scenarios.

references/eClassifiers/PainPoint/index..title: PainPoint
references/eClassifiers/PainPoint/index..content: The problem, frustration, or barrier preventing the user from meeting their goals. Think: ‘What is getting in their way?’ Examples: - Release notes are scattered across tools - Manual steps cause errors - Slow CI pipeline delays deployments - They do not trust the monitoring alerts Pain points are what your product should aim to resolve. Pain points violate needs and block goals. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: products
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type Product Multiplicity 0..* Standalone products not part of a portfolio.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: targetPersonas
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type Persona Multiplicity 0..* Personas this hypothesis is about.

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: painPoints
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type PainPoint Multiplicity 0..* Problems, frustrations, or barriers preventing this persona from meeting their goals.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: owner
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Owner of this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/Edition/referenc.title: includedCapabilities
references/eClassifiers/Edition/referenc.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Capabilities directly included/entitled in this edition.

references/eClassifiers/Action/reference.title: order
references/eClassifiers/Action/reference.content: Type EIntegerObject Multiplicity 0..1 Sequence order of this action within the persona’s workflow.

references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.title: targetGeographies
references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.content: Type Geography Multiplicity 0..* Target geographies for this ICP.

references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.title: regulatoryConstraints
references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.content: Type RegulatoryConstraint Multiplicity 0..* Regulatory constraints applicable to this geography.

references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ind.title: Subscription
references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ind.content: Subscriptions define how the user pays and for how long. Examples: - Monthly vs yearly - Per-user vs per-device - Pay-as-you-go vs committed - Auto-renewal terms - Usage-based or hybrid pricing A subscription describes pricing and time, not features. However, a subscription may also define service levels — for example, LLM models available, token budget, response time. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.title: versions
references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.content: Type Version Multiplicity 0..* Versions of this product.

references/eClassifiers/Action/reference.title: relatedCapabilities
references/eClassifiers/Action/reference.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Capabilities exercised or required by this action.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: lastUpdatedAt
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type EDate Multiplicity 0..1 Date when this hypothesis was last updated.

references/eClassifiers/Edition/referenc.title: availableGeographies
references/eClassifiers/Edition/referenc.content: Type Geography Multiplicity 0..* Geographies where this edition is available.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: capabilityProviders
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type CapabilityProvider Multiplicity 0..* Capability providers in this model.

references/eClassifiers/Alternative/inde.title: Alternative
references/eClassifiers/Alternative/inde.content: A competitor or incumbent solution against which a value proposition is differentiated. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.title: channels
references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.content: Type Channel Multiplicity 0..* Route-to-market channels in this segment.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: subscriptions
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type Subscription Multiplicity 0..* Subscription plans in this model.

references/eClassifiers/Evidence/referen.title: evidenceType
references/eClassifiers/Evidence/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Type of evidence, e.g. ‘metric’, ‘experiment’, ‘testimonial’, ‘case-study’.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: assumptions
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Key assumptions underlying this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/OutcomeMetric/re.title: unit
references/eClassifiers/OutcomeMetric/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Unit of measurement, e.g. ‘percent’, ‘seconds’, ‘count’.

references/eClassifiers/Channel/referenc.title: channelType
references/eClassifiers/Channel/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Type of channel, e.g. ‘Direct’, ‘Self-serve’, ‘Partner’, ‘Reseller’, ‘Marketplace’.

references/eClassifiers/OutcomeMetric/re.title: targetValue
references/eClassifiers/OutcomeMetric/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Target value for this metric, e.g. ‘>90%’, ‘<5s’.

references/eClassifiers/CapabilityProvid.title: versions
references/eClassifiers/CapabilityProvid.content: Type Version Multiplicity 0..* Versions of this capability provider.

references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.title: releases
references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.content: Type Release Multiplicity 0..* Releases of this product.

references/eClassifiers/CapabilityProvid.title: CapabilityProvider
references/eClassifiers/CapabilityProvid.content: Something providing a capability. Example: OpenAI GPT-4o model for AI chat. Capability providers may require other capabilities — forming a directed acyclic graph. There may be multiple capability providers for a single capability. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: evidence
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type Evidence Multiplicity 0..* Evidence that validates or invalidates this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: statement
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Structured belief statement. Example: ‘We believe that [persona] will [outcome] if we [action].’

references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.title: features
references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.content: Type Feature Multiplicity 0..* Features belonging to this product.

references/eClassifiers/Release/referenc.title: version
references/eClassifiers/Release/referenc.content: Type Version Multiplicity 0..1 The version associated with this release.

references/eClassifiers/ModelElement/ref.title: id
references/eClassifiers/ModelElement/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Unique identifier for this element.

references/eClassifiers/Capability/refer.title: versions
references/eClassifiers/Capability/refer.content: Type Version Multiplicity 0..* Versions of this capability.

references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.title: editions
references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.content: Type Edition Multiplicity 0..* Different flavors/editions of this product, e.g. Community, Professional, Enterprise.

references/eClassifiers/ServiceLevel/ind.title: ServiceLevel
references/eClassifiers/ServiceLevel/ind.content: Defines a service level within a subscription. Examples: LLM models available, token budget, response time guarantees. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/CompanySize/refe.title: revenueBand
references/eClassifiers/CompanySize/refe.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Revenue band, e.g. ‘<$1M’, ‘$1M-$10M’, ‘$10M-$100M’, ‘$100M+’.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: supportingEvidence
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type Evidence Multiplicity 0..* Evidence supporting this value proposition.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: linkedGoals
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type Goal Multiplicity 0..* Goals linked to this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: supportedGoals
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type Goal Multiplicity 0..* Goals this value proposition supports.

references/eClassifiers/Channel/index.ht.title: Channel
references/eClassifiers/Channel/index.ht.content: A route-to-market channel. Examples: Direct, Self-serve, Partner/Reseller, Marketplace (e.g. Azure, AWS). Diagram

references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.title: industries
references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.content: Type Industry Multiplicity 0..* Industries within this market segment.

references/eClassifiers/Version/referenc.title: versionScheme
references/eClassifiers/Version/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The versioning scheme, e.g. ‘semantic’, ‘calendar’, ‘incremental’.

references/eClassifiers/RegulatoryConstr.title: regulation
references/eClassifiers/RegulatoryConstr.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Name of the regulation, e.g. ‘GDPR’, ‘HIPAA’, ‘SOC 2’.

references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.title: targetIndustries
references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.content: Type Industry Multiplicity 0..* Target industries for this ICP.

references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.title: segmentType
references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Type of segment, e.g. ‘SMB’, ‘Mid-market’, ‘Enterprise’, ‘Horizontal’, ‘Vertical’.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: mitigatedPainPoints
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type PainPoint Multiplicity 0..* Pain points this value proposition mitigates.

references/eClassifiers/Behavior/referen.title: relatedPainPoints
references/eClassifiers/Behavior/referen.content: Type PainPoint Multiplicity 0..* Pain points related to or caused by this behavior (behavioral friction).

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: role
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Job title or role of the persona, e.g. ‘DevOps Engineer’, ‘Hiring Manager’.

references/eClassifiers/Evidence/referen.title: collectedInSegments
references/eClassifiers/Evidence/referen.content: Type MarketSegment Multiplicity 0..* Market segments in which this evidence was collected.

references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.title: personaAssociations
references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.content: Type FeaturePersonaAssociation Multiplicity 0..* Associations between this feature and specific personas. The association class carries a description and is contained by the feature — features are created to make personas happy.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: alternatives
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type Alternative Multiplicity 0..* Competitor and alternative solutions tracked in this model.

references/eClassifiers/ProductPortfolio.title: products
references/eClassifiers/ProductPortfolio.content: Type Product Multiplicity 0..* Products in this portfolio.

references/eClassifiers/ModelElement/ref.title: description
references/eClassifiers/ModelElement/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Detailed description of this element. May contain Markdown.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: createdAt
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type EDate Multiplicity 0..1 Date when this hypothesis was created.

references/eClassifiers/Release/referenc.title: includedFeatures
references/eClassifiers/Release/referenc.content: Type Feature Multiplicity 0..* Features included in this release.

references/eClassifiers/HypothesisStatus.title: Unvalidated
references/eClassifiers/HypothesisStatus.content: The hypothesis has not yet been tested.

references/eClassifiers/Geography/index..title: Geography
references/eClassifiers/Geography/index..content: A geographic definition — region, country, state/province. Can also include data residency region. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: evidence
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type Evidence Multiplicity 0..* Evidence items in this model.

references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.title: addressedNeeds
references/eClassifiers/Feature/referenc.content: Type Need Multiplicity 0..* Needs that this feature addresses or satisfies.

references/eClassifiers/Industry/referen.title: code
references/eClassifiers/Industry/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Industry classification code, e.g. NAICS or SIC code.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: linkedPainPoints
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type PainPoint Multiplicity 0..* Pain points linked to this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: needs
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type Need Multiplicity 0..* Fundamental requirements this persona has to achieve something meaningful. Needs are often unstated and discovered through research.

references/eClassifiers/Goal/index.html.title: Goal
references/eClassifiers/Goal/index.html.content: The desired outcome the user is trying to accomplish. Think: ‘What is the user trying to achieve?’ Goals may be: - Functional (ship releases faster) - Emotional (feel confident in a deployment) - Aspirational (reduce on-call burden over time) Examples: - Ship a stable release with minimal downtime - Reduce troubleshooting from hours to minutes - Automate repetitive tasks A need is a requirement; a goal is an outcome. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: behaviors
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type Behavior Multiplicity 0..* Behavioral patterns of this persona — habitual, preference-based, and cross-situational patterns describing how the persona typically operates.

references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.title: dataResidencyRegion
references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Data residency region, if different from geographic region.

references/eClassifiers/Behavior/index.h.title: Behavior
references/eClassifiers/Behavior/index.h.content: A consistent, characteristic pattern in how a persona operates. Behaviors are habitual, preference-based, and cross-situational. They capture: - Attitudes (risk-averse, data-driven, collaborative) - Work style (async vs synchronous, detail-oriented, shortcut-taking) - Motivation drivers (speed, quality, confidence, accuracy) - Decision inclinations (consult peers, trust AI, check data first) - Interaction patterns (reads docs, prefers autocomplete, avoids manual steps) Examples: - ‘Checks results multiple times before making a decision’ - ‘Prefers automation over manual review’ Behaviors help design how the experience should feel. They guide design principles. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.title: idealCustomerProfiles
references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.content: Type IdealCustomerProfile Multiplicity 0..* Ideal customer profiles within this market segment.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: status
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type HypothesisStatus Multiplicity 0..1 Current validation status of this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/Behavior/referen.title: relatedNeeds
references/eClassifiers/Behavior/referen.content: Type Need Multiplicity 0..* Needs related to or driven by this behavior.

references/eClassifiers/HypothesisStatus.title: Validated
references/eClassifiers/HypothesisStatus.content: The hypothesis has been validated by evidence.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: differentiatedFromAlternatives
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type Alternative Multiplicity 0..* Alternatives (competitors/incumbents) from which this value proposition is differentiated.

references/eClassifiers/Evidence/referen.title: source
references/eClassifiers/Evidence/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Source of the evidence, e.g. a study name, customer name, experiment ID.

references/eClassifiers/Release/index.ht.title: Release
references/eClassifiers/Release/index.ht.content: A version made available to users. A release references a specific version and tracks when it was published and its current status. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: ProductManagementModel
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Root container for the product management model. Contains all top-level entities. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/in.title: MarketSegment
references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/in.content: A market segment, e.g. SMB, Mid-market, Enterprise; or horizontal vs vertical segments. Markets drive which personas you prioritize, which value props you emphasize, and how you package/price. Market attributes should be kept independent and composable. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.title: region
references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Geographic region, e.g. ‘EMEA’, ‘North America’, ‘APAC’.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: packagedInEditions
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type Edition Multiplicity 0..* Editions in which this value proposition is packaged.

references/eClassifiers/Product/index.ht.title: Product
references/eClassifiers/Product/index.ht.content: A collection of features targeting a specific persona or a subset of personas. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Evidence/referen.title: measuredMetrics
references/eClassifiers/Evidence/referen.content: Type OutcomeMetric Multiplicity 0..* Outcome metrics that this evidence measures.

references/eClassifiers/PainPoint/refere.title: relatedPainPoints
references/eClassifiers/PainPoint/refere.content: Type PainPoint Multiplicity 0..* Other pain points related to this one. Supports shared pain points across personas.

references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.title: targetPersonas
references/eClassifiers/MarketSegment/re.content: Type Persona Multiplicity 0..* Personas targeted by this market segment.

references/eClassifiers/GoalType/referen.title: Emotional
references/eClassifiers/GoalType/referen.content: A feeling-oriented outcome, e.g. ‘feel confident in a deployment’.

references/eClassifiers/Goal/references/.title: relatedGoals
references/eClassifiers/Goal/references/.content: Type Goal Multiplicity 0..* Other goals related to this goal. Supports shared goals across personas.

references/eClassifiers/Release/referenc.title: status
references/eClassifiers/Release/referenc.content: Type ReleaseStatus Multiplicity 0..1 Current status of the release.

references/eClassifiers/Goal/references/.title: goalType
references/eClassifiers/Goal/references/.content: Type GoalType Multiplicity 0..1 The type of goal — functional, emotional, or aspirational.

references/eClassifiers/Persona/index.ht.title: Persona
references/eClassifiers/Persona/index.ht.content: A fictional but research-based representation of a target user. Purpose: to help the product team visualize who they are building for. Includes demographics, behaviors, motivations, context (work environment, tools), and attitudes/preferences. Example: ‘Alex, a 34-year-old DevOps engineer who values automation and hates manual workflows.’ Personas have needs, goals, and pain points. In this model they are contained by a persona for simplicity. Shared needs, goals, or pain points are addressed by reference subtypes or ‘related to’ references. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Need/index.html.title: Need
references/eClassifiers/Need/index.html.content: The fundamental requirement a user has to achieve something meaningful. Think: ‘What must be true for this user to succeed?’ Needs are often unstated, discovered through research. They are not tasks and not goals. They are the underlying necessities. Examples: - Needs a reliable way to track deployment failures - Needs visibility into system performance - Needs a workflow that integrates with existing tools Needs support goals. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.title: serviceLevels
references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.content: Type ServiceLevel Multiplicity 0..* Service level definitions for this subscription, e.g. token budgets, response times, available models.

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: targetSegments
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type MarketSegment Multiplicity 0..* Market segments this value proposition targets (in addition to personas).

references/eClassifiers/Version/referenc.title: date
references/eClassifiers/Version/referenc.content: Type EDate Multiplicity 0..1 Date this version was defined.

references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.title: targetPersonas
references/eClassifiers/Product/referenc.content: Type Persona Multiplicity 0..* Personas this product is targeting.

references/eClassifiers/Action/index.htm.title: Action
references/eClassifiers/Action/index.htm.content: A concrete, observable step a persona performs in a workflow or scenario. Actions are contextual, sequential, and task-level. They capture: - Task steps in a workflow - Interactions with tools or systems - Triggered events (what they do next) - User journey touchpoints Examples: - ‘Uploads a resume file’ - ‘Filters candidates by skill score’ Actions help design what the user must do to succeed. They guide workflow and feature design. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Capability/index.title: Capability
references/eClassifiers/Capability/index.content: Ability to achieve something. Examples: AI chat, embeddings generation, PDF export. Capabilities are used by features. A Feature is a type of Capability. Capabilities may be provided by one or more CapabilityProviders. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Evidence/index.h.title: Evidence
references/eClassifiers/Evidence/index.h.content: Metrics, experiments, testimonials, or case studies that validate a value proposition. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Action/reference.title: relatedFeatures
references/eClassifiers/Action/reference.content: Type Feature Multiplicity 0..* Features involved in or supporting this action.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: marketSegments
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type MarketSegment Multiplicity 0..* Market segments defined in this model.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: valuePropositions
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type ValueProposition Multiplicity 0..* Value propositions defined in this model.

references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.title: techStack
references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Technology stack requirements, e.g. ‘AWS’, ‘Kubernetes’, ‘Java’.

references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.title: targetPersonas
references/eClassifiers/IdealCustomerPro.content: Type Persona Multiplicity 0..* Personas that characterize this ICP.

references/eClassifiers/Feature/index.ht.title: Feature
references/eClassifiers/Feature/index.ht.content: A unit of utility for one or more personas — something that helps to achieve a goal, satisfies a need, or addresses a pain point. One feature may be associated with multiple goals, needs, and pain points and vice versa. Features can be associated with personas directly for coarse-grained models. A Feature is modeled as a subtype of Capability because a feature is itself a capability exposed to users. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Need/references/.title: supportedGoals
references/eClassifiers/Need/references/.content: Type Goal Multiplicity 0..* Goals that this need supports. Fulfilling the need contributes to achieving these goals.

references/eClassifiers/ServiceLevel/ref.title: value
references/eClassifiers/ServiceLevel/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The value or threshold for the metric.

references/eClassifiers/CompanySize/inde.title: CompanySize
references/eClassifiers/CompanySize/inde.content: Employee count bands and/or revenue bands. Example: ‘1-50 employees’, ‘$1M-$10M revenue’. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.title: statement
references/eClassifiers/ValueProposition.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The concise value proposition statement.

references/eClassifiers/GoalType/referen.title: Aspirational
references/eClassifiers/GoalType/referen.content: A long-term, strategic outcome, e.g. ‘reduce on-call burden over time’.

references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.title: hypotheses
references/eClassifiers/ProductManagemen.content: Type Hypothesis Multiplicity 0..* Hypotheses defined in this model.

references/eClassifiers/PainPoint/refere.title: violatedNeeds
references/eClassifiers/PainPoint/refere.content: Type Need Multiplicity 0..* Needs that this pain point violates or undermines.

references/eClassifiers/ProductPortfolio.title: ProductPortfolio
references/eClassifiers/ProductPortfolio.content: A collection of related products managed as a group. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.title: billingCycle
references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Billing cycle, e.g. ‘monthly’, ‘yearly’, ‘pay-as-you-go’.

references/eClassifiers/RegulatoryConstr.title: RegulatoryConstraint
references/eClassifiers/RegulatoryConstr.content: A regulatory constraint determined by industry and/or geography. Governs which capabilities and editions are enabled in specific contexts. Examples: GDPR, HIPAA, SOC 2, data residency requirements. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.title: toolsUsed
references/eClassifiers/Persona/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Tools the persona currently uses in their workflow.

references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.title: enabledByCapabilities
references/eClassifiers/Hypothesis/refer.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Capabilities that would enable validation of this hypothesis.

references/eClassifiers/Version/referenc.title: versionString
references/eClassifiers/Version/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The version string, e.g. ‘1.2.3’, ‘2024.01’.

references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.title: editions
references/eClassifiers/Subscription/ref.content: Type Edition Multiplicity 0..* Editions sold through this subscription plan.

references/eClassifiers/Edition/referenc.title: availableChannels
references/eClassifiers/Edition/referenc.content: Type Channel Multiplicity 0..* Channels through which this edition is available, e.g. Direct, Self-serve, Marketplace.

references/eClassifiers/CapabilityProvid.title: requiredCapabilities
references/eClassifiers/CapabilityProvid.content: Type Capability Multiplicity 0..* Capabilities required by this provider (forms a directed acyclic graph).

references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.title: stateProvince
references/eClassifiers/Geography/refere.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 State or province within the country.
